Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Tree: SEQUENCE of receive_order check_inventory xor_node close_order. xor_node has children: successful_subtree PARALLEL of authorize_payment package_items ship_order and cancel_subtree SEQUENCE cancel_order close_order.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus after check_inventory we go to xor_node: either take successful_subtree or cancel_subtree. Then after xor_node we go to close_order. So after the xor node we always execute close_order.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In cancel_subtree we already have close_order as its second child. So the trace would be: receive_order check_inventory cancel_order close_order from cancel_subtree then close_order roots final. So two close_order events.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: successful_subtree includes authorize_payment package_items ship_order in any order parallel. The parallel operator requires all children to finish before proceeding.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Domain expects: if inventory sufficient: Authorize Payment Package Items Ship Order then Close Order. So they require sequence: authorize_payment package_items ship_order close_order.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The models parallel allows any interleaving e.g. ship_order before authorize_payment or package_items before authorize_payment. So a trace like: receive_order check_inventory ship_order package_items authorize_payment close_order is permitted but violates domain.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Thats a trace: receive_order check_inventory cancel_order close_order close_order. That is invalid because you close order twice.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Choose one that clearly violates domain. Probably the shipping before payment is more illustrative. Well give the shipping before payment example.
